confirmed.title: HasManyReadonly empty-state hardcoded English 'No {relatedResource} linked.'
confirmed.detail: The empty-state paragraph renders the literal `No {f.props.relatedResource} linked.` directly in JSX. This is a visible user-facing string with no translation. The component imports no translator (no useArqelTranslations / useArqelLocale) at all, so it cannot be localized. It IS a live, registered field renderer (register.ts:38 registerField('HasManyReadonly', HasManyReadonly)). Fix: inject useArqelTranslations and replace with a t() call using an interpolated resource name, e.g. t('arqel.fields.has_many_empty', 'No {resource} linked.', { resource: f.props.relatedResource }). NOT in the rounds 1-10 ledger (ledger covers BelongsToInput/MultiSelect/etc. but not HasManyReadonly).
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-js/src/relationship/HasManyReadonly.tsx:25 — the empty-state paragraph renders the literal `No {f.props.relatedResource} linked.` directly in JSX. The component imports only type definitions (HasManyFieldSchema, FieldRendererProps); it has no useArqelTranslations / useArqelLocale / translator import, so the string cannot be localized. It is user-facing (rendered when a has-many relation has zero linked records). Not vendored: path is packages-js/fields-js/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: scanned the full R1-R10 ledger — no entry references HasManyReadonly.tsx or relationship/HasManyReadonly. Round 4 covered sibling relationship/select renderers (BelongsToInput.tsx:80, MultiSelectInput.tsx:50) but not this file. Genuine, novel, user-facing i18n defect.

confirmed.title: Built-in DeleteAction modal heading hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: In packages/actions/src/Actions.php:41 the framework's Actions::delete() factory seeds ->modalHeading('Delete record?') — a raw English string rendered directly in the confirmation modal shown to every end user. The Confirmable trait passes this through verbatim (no trans()). Framework-hardcoded: the framework ships this default, so it must be keyed in actions.php lang, not left to the app. (Path corrected: real file is packages/actions/src/Actions.php.)
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED real, novel, user-facing.

Reproduces: packages/actions/src/Actions.php delete() factory seeds raw English literals: ->modalHeading('Delete record?') (line 41) and ->modalDescription('This action cannot be undone.'). These are framework-shipped defaults rendered in the confirmation modal every end user sees when deleting a record via the built-in delete action.

User-facing in other locales: Confirmable::getConfirmationConfig() does pass these through localizeConfirmable() which calls __($value). However __('Delete record?') is NOT a registered translation key (the lang files packages/core/resources/lang/{en,pt_BR}/actions.php contain only verb labels + confirm.submit/confirm.cancel — no heading/description keys). So __() returns the literal verbatim; in pt_BR the built-in delete modal still renders 'Delete record?' / 'This action cannot be undone.' This is a genuine framework i18n gap: the heading/description should be keyed (e.g. arqel::actions.confirm.delete_heading) and seeded as a key, mirroring how submit/cancel were keyed.

Finding's wording is slightly imprecise ('no trans()' — there IS a __() pass), but the substantive defect is correct and load-bearing: hardcoded English defaults with no corresponding lang key. Path correction to Actions.php is valid.

NOT a duplicate of R1-R10: ledger has packages/actions/src/Concerns/Confirmable.php:32 ('framework hardcodes English Confirm/Cancel BUTTON labels' — that fix added confirm.submit/confirm.cancel keys, not the heading) and Action.php:327 (label/tooltip/successNotification/failureNotification serialized verbatim — different strings). No ledger entry covers the delete() factory's modalHeading/modalDescription seeded defaults. Heading entries in ledger are FormSection/Widget/CommandPalette — different surfaces. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (PHP framework code).

confirmed.title: Built-in DeleteAction modal description hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: packages/actions/src/Actions.php:42 ->modalDescription('This action cannot be undone.') — hardcoded English serialized to the delete confirmation modal. Framework-hardcoded; no corresponding key exists in packages/core/resources/lang/en/actions.php (which only has verb labels + confirm.submit/cancel).
confirmed.why_real:
Finding reproduces (file path corrected to Actions.php, not Action.php). The framework's built-in action factory Actions::delete() (packages/actions/src/Actions.php:41-42) and Actions::deleteBulk() (lines 71-72) seed hardcoded English ->modalHeading('Delete record?') and ->modalDescription('This action cannot be undone.'). These flow through Confirmable::getConfirmationConfig() -> localizeConfirmable() (Confirmable.php:127,145-154), which resolves a string against __() only if it is a registered translation KEY, otherwise returns it verbatim. Since the factory passes literal English prose (not a key like arqel::actions.confirm.cannot_undo), it serializes verbatim into the React confirmation modal (description field) — user-facing in every non-English admin panel. Notably packages/core/resources/lang/en/messages.php:18 and arqel.php:81 already define a 'cannot_undo' => 'This action cannot be undone.' key the factory fails to reference, confirming the localization path exists but is unused by the built-in.

NOT a duplicate: ledger (229 entries, R1-R10) covers Confirmable.php:32 (Confirm/Cancel BUTTON labels — a different field path, already lazy-localized via the __() default at lines 133/136) and Action.php:327 (user-supplied $label/tooltip/notifications). Neither covers the framework-seeded modalHeading/modalDescription literals in the Actions factory. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (PHP source in packages/actions). Genuinely novel, user-facing, framework-hardcoded.

confirmed.title: Built-in DeleteAction modal submit button label hardcoded
confirmed.detail: packages/actions/src/Actions.php:44 ->modalSubmitButtonLabel('Delete') — hardcoded English on the destructive confirm button. Framework-hardcoded; the actions.php lang file has a 'delete' verb key but it is not applied here.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified genuine, user-facing, framework-shipped i18n defect. The built-in Actions::delete() factory (packages/actions/src/Actions.php:44) and Actions::deleteBulk() (:74) hardcode ->modalSubmitButtonLabel('Delete'). This value flows through Confirmable::getConfirmationConfig() (line 132) into localizeConfirmable() (Confirmable.php:145), which calls __('Delete'). Because 'Delete' is a raw literal and not a registered translation key (the real keys are arqel::actions.delete and arqel::actions.confirm.submit), Laravel's __() returns the string unchanged. Confirmed pt_BR lang file packages/core/resources/lang/pt_BR/actions.php has 'delete' => 'Excluir' (line 8) and confirm.submit => 'Confirmar' (line 15), both unreachable here. Net effect: the destructive delete confirm button renders English 'Delete' in a pt_BR (or any non-English) panel.

NOT a duplicate: the finding's primary citation 'Action.php:44' is wrong (that line is the COLOR_WARNING constant), but it also gives the correct 'Actions.php:44' location, correct method, and correct literal, and the defect reproduces there. The only related ledger entry (Round 1, Confirmable.php:32, 'framework hardcodes English Confirm/Cancel button labels') concerns the DEFAULT keyed fallback path (lines 133/136, __('arqel::actions.confirm.submit')/.cancel) used when no explicit label is set — that mechanism works correctly. This finding is the distinct case of the built-in Delete/DeleteBulk factory actions passing a non-keyed literal that bypasses the working default. No Actions.php entry exists anywhere in R1-R10.

NOT vendored: this is PHP framework code in packages/actions, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: Built-in DeleteBulkAction modal heading hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: packages/actions/src/Actions.php:71 ->modalHeading('Delete selected records?') — hardcoded English in the bulk-delete confirmation modal. Framework-hardcoded; no lang key.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified the string exists: packages/actions/src/Actions.php:71 has `->modalHeading('Delete selected records?')` in the framework-shipped `Actions::deleteBulk()` factory helper (also `Actions::delete()` line 41 'Delete record?' and the 'This action cannot be undone.' descriptions). The finding's headline cites Action.php:71 which is a typo (that line is `protected string $variant` — a property decl), but the body correctly cites Actions.php:71 with the exact load-bearing string, and the defect reproduces there.

This IS a genuine user-facing i18n gap. modalHeading values flow through Confirmable::getConfirmationConfig() → localizeConfirmable() → __($value) at serialization (Confirmable.php:126,145). Because 'Delete selected records?' is a literal English sentence and not a registered lang key, __() returns it verbatim, so a non-English panel using the built-in bulk-delete action renders hardcoded English. Contrast the submit/cancel buttons (Confirmable.php:131-136) which correctly fall back to keys arqel::actions.confirm.submit/cancel — the default headings/descriptions were never given equivalent lang keys.

Not a duplicate: the only actions-package ledger entry near this (round 1, Confirmable.php:32) covered the 'Confirm'/'Cancel' BUTTON labels and added the localizeConfirmable pass-through; it did not touch the default factory headings. Actions.php is not in the ledger at all. Not vendored — it is framework PHP, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Confidence is med rather than high only because the headline file path is wrong (Action.php vs Actions.php) and one could argue the round-1 Confirmable localization mechanism was the 'intended' fix point; but the concrete remaining defect — built-in default headings shipped as English literals instead of lang keys — is real, reproducible, novel, and user-facing.

confirmed.title: Built-in DeleteBulkAction modal description + submit label hardcoded
confirmed.detail: packages/actions/src/Actions.php:72-74 ->modalDescription('This action cannot be undone.') and ->modalSubmitButtonLabel('Delete') on the bulk-delete action — hardcoded English serialized to the confirmation modal. Framework-hardcoded; no lang keys.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed real, novel, user-facing. The finding's citation is imprecise (it names packages/actions/src/Action.php:72, but the actual code lives in packages/actions/src/Actions.php). The substance reproduces: the framework's built-in Actions::delete() (Actions.php:41-44) and Actions::deleteBulk() (Actions.php:71-74) hardcode ->modalDescription('This action cannot be undone.') and ->modalSubmitButtonLabel('Delete') as raw English literals, not arqel::actions.* translation keys. These flow through Confirmable::getConfirmationConfig() -> localizeConfirmable() which calls __('This action cannot be undone.'). Because no registered translation namespace/key matches that literal English sentence (the actions package ships NO non-vendor lang directory and the documented pattern is arqel::actions.confirm.* keys, used only for the submit/cancel fallbacks), __() returns the string verbatim. Result: the default row-delete and bulk-delete confirmation modals always render English description + "Delete" submit label regardless of app locale, with no clean per-locale override path. This is user-facing (visible modal text in every Arqel CRUD delete flow).

Not a duplicate: the ledger's two related entries cover different concerns — Confirmable.php:32 (R1) is about the DEFAULT confirm/cancel button fallbacks, which were already fixed to use __('arqel::actions.confirm.submit'/'cancel'); Action.php:327 (R1) covers $label/tooltip/successNotification/failureNotification, not the modal description/submit label on the built-in delete actions. No ledger entry targets Actions.php's hardcoded delete/deleteBulk modal strings.

Not a vendored shadcn primitive — it is server-side PHP in packages/actions/src/Actions.php.

Confidence med: the defect clearly reproduces, but there is a partial mitigation path (a developer could register a translation keyed by the exact English string), so severity is moderate rather than absolute; the i18n gap is nonetheless genuine since the framework ships no such key and the locale-driven path does not work out of the box.

confirmed.title: VersionHistoryController 'Resource [..] not registered' hardcoded
confirmed.detail: Line 60 returns JsonResponse(['message' => "Resource [{$resource}] not registered"], 404) with raw English interpolation. A matching key ALREADY EXISTS at packages/core/resources/lang/en/messages.php:60 ('resource_not_registered' => 'Resource [:resource] not registered') but is not used here — round-9/10 keyed the sibling 'registry_not_bound'/'Forbidden' (ledger L49) yet missed this dynamic-slug branch. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages/versioning/src/Http/Controllers/VersionHistoryController.php:60: returns JsonResponse(['message' => "Resource [{$resource}] not registered"], 404) with raw English interpolation, while sibling branches (lines 49/53) route through the $this->message() helper (defined at line 154). A matching translation key already exists at packages/core/resources/lang/en/messages.php:60 ('resource_not_registered' => 'Resource [:resource] not registered') but is unused here, so the fix is trivial and feasible. User-facing: this 404 JSON 'message' is returned to the client when a resource slug is unregistered. NOT a duplicate: the round-9 ledger entry for this file is line 49 only ('ResourceRegistry not bound' / 'Forbidden'); it explicitly did not cover the line-60 dynamic-slug (findBySlug===null) branch. The round-9 AI-controller entry covering the identical string is a different file (AiGenerateController.php:42), not this controller. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (lives in packages/versioning, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*).

confirmed.title: VersionHistoryController 'Resource [..] is invalid' hardcoded
confirmed.detail: Line 64 returns JsonResponse(['message' => "Resource [{$resource}] is invalid"], 404) — raw English, no trans(). No lang key currently exists for this; needs a new versioning.resource_invalid key. Framework-hardcoded; sibling branch (L60) also missed.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at packages/versioning/src/Http/Controllers/VersionHistoryController.php:64 — the `! method_exists($resourceClass, 'getModel')` branch returns `new JsonResponse(['message' => "Resource [{$resource}] is invalid"], 404)`, a raw English string with no trans()/__()/$this->message() wrapper. This reaches the client verbatim as a user-facing 404 JSON message. Sibling branches in the same method (lines 49/53, registry-not-bound) were already converted to `$this->message('arqel::messages.versioning.registry_not_bound', ...)`, confirming the localization helper exists and is the established pattern here — line 64 was simply missed.

Not a duplicate: the only VersionHistoryController entry in the R1-R10 ledger is round 9, line 49 ("ResourceRegistry not bound / Forbidden"). That fix touched the registry branch only. Line 64 ("is invalid") is a distinct branch, has no corresponding lang key (grep of versioning/ and core/resources/lang found only `resource_not_registered`, never an `is_invalid`/`resource_invalid` key), and appears nowhere in the ledger. The finding's note that the L60 sibling is also unfixed is consistent with the evidence but L64 is the novel subject.

Not vendored: file is in packages/versioning, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Genuinely reproduces, novel, user-facing.

confirmed.title: Marketplace controllers 'Plugin [slug] not found' hardcoded (10 sites)
confirmed.detail: A systemic missed family: JsonResponse(['message' => "Plugin [{$slug}] not found"], 404) is hardcoded in raw English (interpolated slug) across PluginDetailController:29, PluginReviewListController:27, PluginReviewController:50, PluginAdminReviewController:45, PluginFeatureController:42, PluginDownloadController:31, PluginPurchaseController:36, PluginPurchaseController:128, AdminRefundController:31. Prior rounds keyed the 'forbidden'/'unauthenticated'/'validation_failed'/'purchase_not_found'/'review_not_found' messages in these same files (ledger) but the dynamic plugin-not-found branch was missed everywhere. No 'plugin_not_found' key exists in the marketplace lang section. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real: Verified reproduction: "Plugin [{$slug}] not found" is hardcoded raw English in a 404 JsonResponse 'message' at PluginDetailController:29 and 9 other marketplace controller sites (PluginReviewListController:27, PluginReviewController:50, PluginPurchaseController:36 & :128, PluginFeatureController:42, PluginAdminReviewController:45, PluginDownloadController:31, AdminRefundController:31). Confirmed via grep; no plugin_not_found lang key exists in the marketplace package. This is user-facing (API 404 JSON returned to clients) and never passes through __(). Not in the R1-R10 ledger: round 9 keyed the OTHER hardcoded messages in these same files (Forbidden, Unauthenticated, Validation failed, Purchase/Review not found, License required, Refund failed, Payment verification failed) but the dynamic plugin-not-found branch was missed at every site. Framework-authored controllers under packages/marketplace/src/, not a vendored shadcn primitive. Genuinely novel, real, user-facing defect within an incompletely-swept family.

confirmed.title: PluginsByCategoryController 'Category [slug] not found' hardcoded
confirmed.detail: Line 22 returns JsonResponse(['message' => "Category [{$slug}] not found"], 404) — raw English interpolation, no trans(). No 'category_not_found' key in the marketplace lang section. Framework-hardcoded; same missed not-found family as the Plugin controllers.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages/marketplace/src/Http/Controllers/PluginsByCategoryController.php:22: `return new JsonResponse(['message' => "Category [{$slug}] not found"], 404);` — a raw English interpolated string returned in a 404 JSON API response with no trans()/__() wrapping. This is framework code (packages/marketplace, not a vendored packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/* primitive), user-facing (public marketplace category endpoint), and genuinely reproduces. Ledger scan: no entry exists for PluginsByCategoryController or the 'Category [slug] not found' message in rounds 1-10. Round 9 swept sibling marketplace controllers (PluginFeatureController 'Forbidden', PluginDownloadController 'Unauthenticated'/'License required', PluginPurchaseController 'Validation failed'/'Payment verification failed', AdminRefundController 'Purchase not found'/'Refund failed') but this controller and this not-found message were missed — same family, novel instance. Not vendored, not duplicate.

confirmed.title: CollabDocumentController validation message hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: store() returns JsonResponse(['message' => 'state must be a non-empty base64 string'], 422) at L66 and 'state is not valid base64' at L73 — raw English error messages returned to the collaborative-editor frontend. This entire controller is absent from the ledger (round 10 covered realtime ConnectionStatusBanner only). Framework-hardcoded; no lang keys exist.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages/realtime/src/Http/Controllers/CollabDocumentController.php: store() returns JsonResponse(['message' => 'state must be a non-empty base64 string'], 422) at L66 and ['message' => 'state is not valid base64'], 422 at L73. Both are raw hardcoded English, no __() wrapping, no lang keys. The class docblock confirms this is a REST endpoint (POST /admin/{resource}/{id}/collab/{field}) consumed by the collaborative-editor frontend, so the JSON 'message' key is user-facing (the conventional field a frontend surfaces on a 422). NOT vendored: it lives in packages/realtime/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. NOT a duplicate: scanning the full R1-R10 ledger, this controller file never appears. The only realtime entries (round 5 ConnectionStatusBanner.tsx L29, and no round-10 realtime entry) cover a different file. This finding also matches an established, accepted defect class in the ledger (controller JSON 'message' hardcoded English — e.g. round 5 GlobalActivityLogController, round 6 RecordActivityController/audit, round 9 AI + marketplace controllers), reinforcing it as a genuine framework i18n defect rather than app misuse. High confidence.

confirmed.title: CollabDocumentController 'version conflict' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: On a concurrent-edit collision, store() returns JsonResponse(['message' => 'version conflict', 'serverVersion' => ...], 409) at L110 — hardcoded English shown to end users in the collaborative editor when their save is rejected. Framework-hardcoded; no lang key. This is the most user-visible of the three CollabDocumentController strings.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages/realtime/src/Http/Controllers/CollabDocumentController.php:108-113: store() returns new JsonResponse(['message' => 'version conflict', 'serverVersion' => ...], 409) on optimistic-concurrency collision. The 'version conflict' string is hardcoded English with no __() wrapping and no lang key — framework-shipped, surfaced to end users of the collaborative editor when a concurrent save is rejected. Not vendored: it's a PHP controller in packages/realtime, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: grepping all 10 rounds of /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json, there is no entry for CollabDocumentController or any packages/realtime/...php file. The only realtime entry is round 5 packages-js/realtime/src/ConnectionStatusBanner.tsx — a different (JS client) file. This is consistent with the ledger's established class of confirmed user-facing controller-JSON i18n defects (audit/marketplace/versioning/AI 4xx messages in rounds 5/6/9).

confirmed.title: Checkout money formatter is locale-insensitive (hardcoded '.' decimal, no grouping, fixed currency-code-first order)
confirmed.detail: formatMoney(cents, currency) does `(cents/100).toFixed(2)` then `"${currency} ${value}"`. toFixed always emits a '.' decimal separator and never groups thousands, and the currency code is unconditionally placed before the amount. Under pt-BR a price like 199900 cents renders `BRL 1999.00` instead of `R$ 1.999,00`. Repro: open /plugins/<slug>/checkout with app locale pt_BR — the summary card (lines 42/48/54 call formatMoney) shows English-shaped money. Fix: `new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, { style: 'currency', currency }).format(cents/100)`.
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED. apps/marketplace/resources/js/Pages/Marketplace/Checkout.tsx:16-19 defines formatMoney(cents, currency) as `(cents/100).toFixed(2)` then `${currency} ${value}`. toFixed unconditionally emits a '.' decimal separator and never groups thousands, and the currency code is hardcoded before the amount. This page is rendered in pt-BR (Portuguese UI strings throughout; the marketplace root view hardcodes lang=pt-BR per ledger R6). So 199900 cents renders `BRL 1999.00` instead of the locale-correct `R$ 1.999,00`. The helper feeds three user-facing summary lines (price/fee/total) via data-testid summary-price/fee/total at lines 42/48/54. User-facing and genuinely reproduces.

NOT vendored: this is an app-level Inertia page under apps/marketplace, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

NOT a duplicate of the R1-R10 ledger (229 entries scanned). No entry references Checkout.tsx or its formatMoney helper. The nearest money/locale findings target different files: cells.tsx:169 (NumberCell in packages-js/ui DataTable), CurrencyField.php / CurrencyInput.tsx (fields packages), Pulse blade cards (packages/core), StatCard/TableCard (widgets), and the marketplace entries cover app.blade.php lang attribute (R6) and controller JSON error strings (R7/R9) — none cover this Checkout money formatter. The proposed Intl.NumberFormat fix is correct (modulo needing a locale source threaded into the page).

confirmed.title: CompareTable price formatter is locale-insensitive (same toFixed/'.' money pattern)
confirmed.detail: formatPrice() returns `"${(cents/100).toFixed(2)} ${currency}"` — hardcoded '.' decimal, no thousands grouping, English-shaped. Rendered via the 'price' row (line 22) in the comparison table. Repro: compare two paid plugins under pt_BR; the Preço column shows `19.99 USD` instead of a localized currency string. Fix: Intl.NumberFormat with style:'currency' in the active locale.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/apps/marketplace/resources/js/Components/Marketplace/CompareTable.tsx:14-19. formatPrice() returns `${(cents/100).toFixed(2)} ${currency}` — a locale-insensitive money format: hardcoded '.' decimal separator, no thousands grouping, currency code appended English-shaped. It is rendered via the 'price' row at line 22 (label 'Preço') as visible cell text (values[idx] at line 93). The surrounding UI is PT-BR ('Preço', 'Downloads', 'Estrelas'), so under pt_BR a paid plugin displays e.g. '19.99 USD' instead of a localized 'US$ 19,99' — a genuine user-facing i18n/locale-format defect. Correct fix is Intl.NumberFormat({style:'currency', currency}) under the active locale. NOT in the R1-R10 ledger (no match for CompareTable/formatPrice/marketplace price; ledger entries are all packages-js/ui table/form files). NOT a vendored shadcn primitive — it lives in apps/marketplace application code, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Novel, reproduces, user-facing.

confirmed.title: CompareTable renders raw ISO date string for last release (no locale formatting)
confirmed.detail: The 'last_release' row value is `(p) => p.updated_at ?? '—'` — the server's raw timestamp (e.g. `2026-06-23T14:05:00Z` or `2026-06-23 14:05:00`) is shown verbatim, never formatted for the active locale. No localized day/month presentation; no respect for the user's date order/format. Repro: render the compare table; the Último release cell shows the raw DB/ISO string. Fix: format via `new Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale).format(new Date(p.updated_at))`.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at apps/marketplace/resources/js/Components/Marketplace/CompareTable.tsx:28: the 'last_release' row uses `value: (p) => p.updated_at ?? '—'`, rendering the server's raw timestamp string verbatim with no locale-aware formatting (no Intl.DateTimeFormat, no date helper). The value falls straight into the rendered table cell (line 93, {values[idx]}), so the user sees the raw ISO/DB date with no localized day/month order. This is a genuine user-facing i18n defect in an application component, not a vendored shadcn primitive (it lives under apps/marketplace, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Searched the R1-R10 ledger (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json) for CompareTable, last_release, and updated_at — no match; all ledger entries are in packages-js/ui/src/table/*, form/*, etc. Novel and reproducible.

confirmed.title: PluginCard install-count uses hardcoded English compact suffixes (M/k) and '.' decimal
confirmed.detail: formatInstallCount() returns `"${(count/1_000_000).toFixed(1)}M"` / `"${(count/1_000).toFixed(1)}k"`. The 'M' and 'k' suffixes are English compact notation and the toFixed decimal is always '.'; under pt-BR the compact form and separator differ (e.g. `1,2 mil`). Rendered at line 47 (`{formatInstallCount(...)} installs`). The literal ' installs' label is also hardcoded English. Repro: a plugin with 12,300 installs shows `12.3k installs` regardless of locale. Fix: `new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, { notation: 'compact' }).format(count)` plus a translated unit label.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at apps/marketplace/resources/js/Components/Marketplace/PluginCard.tsx:10-14. formatInstallCount() hardcodes English compact suffixes ('M'/'k') and uses toFixed() which always emits '.' as the decimal separator. Rendered at line 47 as `{formatInstallCount(...)} installs` with the ' installs' label also hardcoded English. A plugin with 12,300 installs renders `12.3k installs` for every locale; pt-BR users should see compact/separator/unit differing (e.g. '1,2 mil instalações'). This is genuinely user-facing and locale-incorrect — and the SAME component already localizes its compare button to pt-BR ('Remover de comparar', 'Limite (3) atingido', 'Adicionar a comparar' at lines 73-76), confirming pt-BR is the intended locale and making the English number formatting + 'installs' label a real defect. The file is an app component, NOT a vendored shadcn primitive under packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. The R1-R10 ledger (i18n-fixed-ledger.json) contains no entry for PluginCard, 'install', 'compact', 'formatInstall', or 'locale-format' — all ledger entries target packages-js/ui/src/* (table/, form/, etc.). Novel, reproduces, user-facing, not vendored.

confirmed.title: PublisherProfile total-downloads uses hardcoded English compact suffixes (M/k)
confirmed.detail: formatNumber() is the same `toFixed(1)` + 'M'/'k' compact-suffix pattern as PluginCard, rendered at line 140 (`{formatNumber(stats.total_downloads)}`). Non-localized compact notation and '.' decimal. Repro: a publisher with 1,500,000 downloads shows `1.5M` under any locale. Fix: Intl.NumberFormat notation:'compact' in the active locale.
confirmed.why_real: Verified in code: apps/marketplace/resources/js/Pages/Marketplace/PublisherProfile.tsx lines 11-15 define formatNumber() using hardcoded English compact suffixes ('M'/'k') and toFixed(1) which forces a '.' decimal separator. It is rendered at line 140 ({formatNumber(stats.total_downloads)}) inside the Downloads stat card. Repro holds: a publisher with 1,500,000 downloads renders '1.5M' under any locale; a pt-BR user (the app already mixes PT-BR, e.g. line 144 'Rating médio') would expect compact notation like '1,5 mi' with a decimal comma. This is genuinely user-facing, non-localized number formatting. Not vendored: the file is an app page under apps/marketplace, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: the R1-R10 ledger (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json) has no entry for this file, for formatNumber, or for the PluginCard sibling. The nearest ledger entries (StatCard.tsx:115, TableCard.tsx:104 in round 9) are different files in packages-js/ui covering raw-number grouping, not this app-level compact-suffix function. Marketplace ledger entries are all PHP controller/blade/request strings, unrelated. Confidence high on real and on non-duplicate; the only proper fix is Intl.NumberFormat with notation:'compact' in the active locale.

confirmed.title: PluginDetail renders raw ISO version-release date (no locale formatting)
confirmed.detail: `{v.released_at}` prints the raw server timestamp for each version in the release list — never localized. No localized date order/month name. Repro: open a plugin detail page; each version row shows the raw `released_at` string (e.g. `2026-06-23T00:00:00Z`). Fix: `new Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale, { dateStyle: 'medium' }).format(new Date(v.released_at))`.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at apps/marketplace/resources/js/Pages/Marketplace/PluginDetail.tsx:119: `{v.released_at}` renders the raw server timestamp (e.g. 2026-06-23T00:00:00Z) directly, with no Intl.DateTimeFormat / toLocaleDateString / locale-aware formatting anywhere in the versions tab. This reproduces as a user-facing defect: the version list on the plugin detail page shows un-localized ISO strings with no localized month name or date order. Not vendored: the file lives under apps/marketplace, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate of rounds 1-10: searched the ledger (229 entries). Zero entries reference PluginDetail.tsx or released_at. The marketplace-tagged ledger entries are distinct files/defects — app.blade.php html lang (round 6), SubmitPluginRequest messages (round 7), and ~8 controller JSON messages (round 9). Other date-format findings (table cells.tsx, versioning VersionTimeline, export FormatsDateCells, demo MainDashboard) are in unrelated files. This specific marketplace version-release date render was never covered.

confirmed.title: PluginAutoChecker emits naive '(s)' pluralization for screenshot count
confirmed.detail: `'message' => "Provided {$count} screenshot(s)."` uses the English '(s)' pluralization hack — it cannot produce a correct singular ('1 screenshot') and is not localizable to languages with different plural rules. The whole PluginAutoChecker service returns hardcoded English check messages (lines 59, 66, 81, 88, 103, 110, 126, 133, 151, 158) consumed by the plugin auto-check report; none go through trans()/trans_choice. Repro: run the auto-checker on a plugin with 1 screenshot — message reads 'Provided 1 screenshot(s).'. Fix: route through `trans_choice('arqel-marketplace::checks.screenshots', $count, ['count' => $count])` with proper {one}/{other} forms, and translate the sibling messages.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed in source: packages/marketplace/src/Services/PluginAutoChecker.php line 133 reads `'message' => "Provided {$count} screenshot(s)."`. This uses the English '(s)' pluralization hack — with $count=1 it renders the grammatically wrong 'Provided 1 screenshot(s).' and cannot satisfy languages with different plural rules. The message is hardcoded English, never routed through trans()/trans_choice(); the same is true for all sibling check messages (lines 59, 66, 81, 88, 103, 110, 126, 133, 151, 158). Per the class docblock these messages feed the plugin-submission controller's check report, so they are user-facing. Not a duplicate: no ledger entry references PluginAutoChecker.php in any of rounds 1-10. Existing marketplace ledger entries cover only SubmitPluginRequest.php (R7) and marketplace HTTP controllers' JSON messages (R9), not this service. The '(s)' hack appears in the ledger for other files (TableToolbar R3, Pulse resources.blade R8, VersionPresenter R10) but never for this file/line. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (PHP service, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Minor caveat: the string only appears on the success ('ok') path, so its user impact is limited to a passing-check confirmation, but it is genuinely user-visible with a real pluralization/localization defect — consistent with the standard the ledger applies elsewhere.
